---
name: assets
description: 'Generate all favicons, icons, OG image, and README banner from your logomark.'
agent: agent
tools: ['runInTerminal']
---

# Generate Brand Assets

Run the asset generator to produce all favicons, icons, OG image, and README banner from your logomark.

## Steps

1. Delete any v0/Vercel placeholder files from `public/` if they exist:

   ```powershell
   $placeholders = @(
     'public/placeholder.jpg', 'public/placeholder.svg',
     'public/placeholder-logo.png', 'public/placeholder-logo.svg',
     'public/placeholder-user.jpg',
     'public/apple-icon.png',
     'public/icon.svg', 'public/icon-dark-32x32.png', 'public/icon-light-32x32.png'
   )
   $placeholders | Where-Object { Test-Path $_ } | ForEach-Object { Remove-Item $_ }
   ```

   Report how many were removed (or "none found" if clean).

2. Check that `public/brand/logomark.png` exists. If it doesn't, stop and tell the user:

   > "Drop your logomark at `public/brand/logomark.png` (1024x1024, transparent background) and re-run `/assets`."

3. Check that ImageMagick is installed by running `magick --version`. If it's not found, stop and tell the user:

   > "ImageMagick is required. Install it from https://imagemagick.org then re-run `/assets`."

4. Run the asset generator:

   ```powershell
   .\scripts\generate-assets.ps1
   ```

5. If the script succeeds, report which files were generated. Note the **favicon mode** in the output (DUAL, AUTO, or SINGLE) -- you'll need it in the next step.

6. **Generate `public/favicon.svg` and wire favicons in `src/app/layout.tsx`**

   Check the favicon mode logged by the script:
   - **DUAL mode** (`logomark-dark.png` found) or **AUTO mode** (grayscale mark, auto-inverted): the two PNG variants are distinct. Generate an SVG favicon that embeds both and toggles between them via CSS `display`. Chrome and Firefox use SVG favicons and respect the `prefers-color-scheme` media query inside the SVG -- this is the only mechanism that actually switches the browser tab icon. The PNG `media` entries are kept as belt-and-suspenders fallbacks but are vestigial in practice.

     ```powershell
     # Resize to 64x64 (browser favicons never render larger than this)
     magick public/icon-light.png -resize 64x64 public/tmp-light.png
     magick public/icon-dark.png -resize 64x64 public/tmp-dark.png
     $b64light = [Convert]::ToBase64String([System.IO.File]::ReadAllBytes("public\tmp-light.png"))
     $b64dark  = [Convert]::ToBase64String([System.IO.File]::ReadAllBytes("public\tmp-dark.png"))
     Remove-Item public/tmp-light.png, public/tmp-dark.png

     $svg = @"
     <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 64 64">
       <style>
         .dark { display: none; }
         @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
           .light { display: none; }
           .dark  { display: block; }
         }
       </style>
       <image class="light" width="64" height="64" href="data:image/png;base64,$b64light"/>
       <image class="dark"  width="64" height="64" href="data:image/png;base64,$b64dark"/>
     </svg>
     "@

     [System.IO.File]::WriteAllText(
       "$PWD\public\favicon.svg",
       $svg,
       (New-Object System.Text.UTF8Encoding($false))
     )
     ```

     Then add an `icons` block to `metadata` in `src/app/layout.tsx`:

     ```ts
     // SVG favicon is primary -- it embeds the logomark with a prefers-color-scheme
     // media query inside the SVG itself, which is how Chrome actually switches icons.
     // PNG fallbacks are kept for browsers that don't support SVG favicons (Safari).
     // Generated by /assets -- re-run if the logomark changes.
     icons: {
       icon: [
         { url: '/favicon.svg', type: 'image/svg+xml' },
         { url: '/icon-light.png', media: '(prefers-color-scheme: light)' },
         { url: '/icon-dark.png', media: '(prefers-color-scheme: dark)' },
       ],
     },
     ```

   - **SINGLE mode** (colored mark, same file for both): generate a single-image SVG with no media query and wire it as the primary icon:

     ```powershell
     magick public/icon-light.png -resize 64x64 public/tmp-icon.png
     $b64 = [Convert]::ToBase64String([System.IO.File]::ReadAllBytes("public\tmp-icon.png"))
     Remove-Item public/tmp-icon.png

     $svg = @"
     <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 64 64">
       <image width="64" height="64" href="data:image/png;base64,$b64"/>
     </svg>
     "@

     [System.IO.File]::WriteAllText(
       "$PWD\public\favicon.svg",
       $svg,
       (New-Object System.Text.UTF8Encoding($false))
     )
     ```

     Then in `src/app/layout.tsx`:

     ```ts
     icons: {
       icon: [
         { url: '/favicon.svg', type: 'image/svg+xml' },
         { url: '/icon-light.png' },
       ],
     },
     ```

   > **Browser behavior summary:**
   >
   > - Chrome / Firefox: use `favicon.svg`, switch on OS color scheme (requires page refresh, not live).
   > - Safari: does not support SVG favicons — falls back to `src/app/favicon.ico` (multi-res ICO, always light mark). Dark-mode favicon switching is not supported in Safari.
   > - iOS "Add to Home Screen": uses `src/app/apple-icon.png` (180×180 with brand background). ✅
   > - Android "Add to Home Screen": uses manifest icon from `src/app/icon.png`. ✅
   > - `src/app/icon.png` must remain — Next.js uses it to auto-generate the webmanifest.

7. Write `README.md` -- overwrite the entire file with the project README following the `## README Standard` format from `.github/copilot-instructions.md`:
   - Banner image line: `![{site.name}](public/brand/banner.png)`
   - H1: product name only
   - Tagline: one sentence -- what the user gets, outcome-focused, no buzzwords
   - Live link: `-> [domain](url)` -- read URL from `src/config/site.ts`
   - `---` divider
   - Stack line: read `package.json` dependencies for the core tech list
   - Nothing else

8. Commit the generated assets and README:
   ```powershell
   git add public/ src/app/apple-icon.png README.md
   git commit -m "assets: generate favicons, icons, banner, and README"
   ```

## Rules

- Never push -- commit only.
- If the script errors, show the full output and stop.
- Do not modify any source files -- this command only runs the generator and commits the output.
